2.Name: Testentry2 - Taoist 15 Dogs, Dominating the Region

3.Name: Testentry3 - Mage's Frost Instant Kill
3.Description: Randomly Reduce Ice Resistance of Half the Monsters

4.Name: Pursue Victory
4.Description: Elite-level monsters receive 35% less damage when in a super armor state, but take 250% damage when weakened.

5.Name: Holy Baptism
5.Description: Within 3s of receiving a Healing, hitting an enemy reduces the CD of all skills by 1s.

6.Name: Prism
6.Description: The number of projectile skills for the character +3, duration +10s, and damage dealt +200%.

7.Name: Barrier
7.Description: The character's shield gain +300%, and while a shield is present, Engram recovery speed +100%.

8.Name: Gale
8.Description: When the character deals Wind damage, there is a 10% chance to create a wind field that lasts for 7s. The wind field continuously deals damage and attracts nearby monsters. Monsters within the wind field take 50% more damage. CD: 10s.

9.Name: Stun
9.Description: When the character deals Fire damage, it adds a 5% increased damage taken effect for 5s, which can stack.

10.Name: Impenetrable Wall
10.Description: When monsters take damage, the damage -500 points.

11.Name: Soul Devourer
11.Description: Each time the character takes damage, it restores 5% of nearby monsters' health.

12.Name: Fight to the Death
12.Description: At the start of the battle, the character's ultimate gauge is filled. When the character is not in a healthy state, they gain a 75% damage increase. When in an injured state, they gain an additional 75% damage increase.

13.Name: Perfectionist
13.Description: When the character is in a healthy state, damage dealt +30%.

14.Name: Wound
14.Description: When a monster hits the character, the damage the character takes is permanently +3%. This effect can stack.

15.Name: Will to Rebirth
15.Description: When a non-boss monster's health drops to 0, it will restore to full health once and continuously take damage.

16.Name: Double-Edged Sword
16.Description: The character gains 1 stack of effect every 5s, each stack increases damage dealt and damage taken by 10% (up to 20 stacks), each time the character takes damage, 1 stack is removed.

17.Name: War Enthusiasm
17.Description: The character takes damage equal to 1% of their Max HP per second (not lethal), and each time a monster is defeated, the character recovers 10% of their Max HP.

18.Name: Dual Wielding
18.Description: After switching weapons, the character gains a 30% increase in damage and Healing for 10s.

19.Name: Frost Blade
19.Description: When a monster hits the character, the character's Speed -10% for 20s, stacking up to 7 times.

20.Name: Bomber
20.Description: When a monster dissipates, it causes a large explosion at its original location.

22.Name: Aura of Devotion
22.Description: After a monster dissipates, it leaves a blazing trap on the ground that deals damage to players for 15s.

23.Name: Upgrade Module
23.Description: Monsters increase damage by 30% every 5s they survive, elite level monsters increase damage by 20% every 10s they survive, and boss monsters increase damage by 10% every 20s they survive.

24.Name: Spirit
24.Description: Monsters gain a shield that grants complete immunity to damage 3 times every 20s.

25.Name: Swift Flash
25.Description: When the character triggers a perfect dodge, all skill CDs are refreshed, CD: 30s.

26.Name: Chaotic Thunder
26.Description: When the character deals damage, there is a 10% chance to summon a lightning strike to hit the enemy (this effect is influenced by engraving probability bonuses).

27.Name: Nuclear Impact
27.Description: When monsters take Crit DMG, the damage value is recorded and reduced to 0. Every 3s, the recorded damage value is settled, and during this period, the recorded damage value +50% per second.

28.Name: Rupture
28.Description: When monsters dissipate, they explode, causing damage to nearby characters.

29.Name: Inspire
29.Description: When monsters dissipate, they permanently increase the damage dealt by other monsters within 5 meters (excluding boss level) by 30% and reduce the damage they take by 10%. This effect can stack, with DMG Reduction increasing up to a maximum of 50%.

30.Name: Pursue Victory
30.Description: Arcane value acquisition efficiency +20%, and after breaking super armor, deal triple damage within 10s.

31.Name: Water Element Shield
31.Description: When monsters spawn, they gain a layer of water element shield, taking 150% Ice damage and 50% other attribute damage.

32.Name: Fire Shield
32.Description: When monsters spawn, they gain a layer of Fire shield, taking 150% Fire damage and 50% other attribute damage

33.Name: Thunder Element Shield
33.Description: Monsters will gain a layer of Water Element Shield upon spawning, taking 150% Thunder Element damage and 50% other attribute damage.

34.Name: Wind Shield
34.Description: Monsters will gain a layer of Water Element Shield upon spawning, taking 150% Wind damage and 50% other attribute damage.

35.Name: Forest Shield
35.Description: Monsters will gain a layer of Water Element Shield upon spawning, taking 150% Forest Damage and 50% other attribute damage.

36.Name: Arena
36.Description: Monsters of elite level or higher will generate a 5-meter diameter Shrine around them, making monsters within the Shrine immune to damage from outside the Shrine.

37.Name: Concealment
37.Description: Monsters have a 10% chance to enter a 3-second invisibility state when attacked, becoming immune to damage, with a 20-second CD after activation.

38.Name: Glass Cannon
38.Description: Character's damage dealt +200%, but Max HP -50%.

39.Name: Spring
39.Description: Monsters will recover 50% of the damage taken as health 0.5s after taking damage.

40.Name: Analgesia
40.Description: When the character takes damage from monsters, the damage value is recorded and reduced to zero. When the total recorded damage exceeds the character's HP, the character immediately dissipates.

41.Name: Venom
41.Description: After dealing damage, monsters will inflict a debuff that reduces Max HP by 1% per second, and up to 10 stacks. This effect is removed if the character is healed to full health.

42.Name: Stormy Weather
42.Description: Each time the character deals damage, an additional 10% of their ATK as non-elemental damage is added.

43.Name: Perfect State
43.Description: When the character's health is above 80%, they deal an additional 20% damage.

44.Name: Void Soul
44.Description: When Tina's HP drops to 70% and 35%, she will summon a Void Soul respectively. The Void Soul will target the player with the highest rage and deal DoT to them. The Void Soul receives 80% DMG Reduction when its super armor is not broken and takes 500% damage when its super armor is broken. When the Void Soul dissipates, it increases all players' damage by 100% for 10s.

45.Name: Stormy Weather
45.Description: Each time the character deals damage, an additional 8% of their ATK as damage is added.

46.Name: Swift Flash
46.Description: When the character triggers a perfect dodge, all skill CDs are refreshed, CD: 10s.

47.Name: Chaotic Thunder
47.Description: When the character lands a Crit, there is a 30% chance to generate a lightning strike that attacks nearby enemies. Each Crit increases the trigger chance by 10%, lasting for 10s, up to a maximum of 100%.

48.Name: Dual Wielding
48.Description: After switching weapons, the character gains a 30% increase in damage and Healing for 10s.

49.Name: Holy Baptism
49.Description: Within 3s of receiving a Healing, hitting an enemy reduces the CD of all skills by 1s.

50.Name: Corrupt Regeneration
50.Description: The Max HP of Monsters -25%, but they recover 5% of their Max HP per second.

51.Name: Affliction
51.Description: Each stack increases damage taken by 5%. Defeating a normal monster adds 1 stack, and defeating an elite monster adds 3 stacks.

52.Name: Reshape
52.Description: Gain dungeon skill: Refresh, consuming 35% of health, refreshes all skill CDs, and increases damage by 50% for 10s. CD: 1 minute.

53.Name: Warning Line
53.Description: Monsters cannot take more than 10% of their Max HP as damage per hit.

54.Name: Wild Surge
54.Description: When a monster within 10 meters of the character is defeated, reduce all skill CDs by 1s.

55.Name: Holy Healing
55.Description: When the character receives healing, it deals equal damage to units within 10 meters.

56.Name: Quick Strike
56.Description: The character's damage dealt -80%, but each time damage is dealt, it increases damage by 2.5% for 5s, stackable up to a maximum of 200%.

57.Name: Isolated
57.Description: When there are no other monsters around a non-boss monster, the damage it receives +80%.

58.Name: Inflatable Hammer
58.Description: The character's damage +30%, but this entry becomes temporarily inactive when the character is in an injured state.

59.Name: Time Bomb
59.Description: During combat, every 25s or when a normal monster dissipates, there is a chance to spawn a self-destructing robot that deals damage to all players after a certain time.

60.Name: Loneliness
60.Description: When there are allies within 10 meters of the character, the character gains a buff that increases damage dealt by 30%.

61.Name: Burning Heart
61.Description: After entering combat, the character enters a burning state every 30s for 10s, during which moving will cause damage based on a percentage of Max HP.

62.Name: Rally
62.Description: After entering combat, every 30s a shield is generated on one player, and after 5s, lightning strikes all players. Those outside the shield take damage equal to 50% of their Max HP.

63.Name: Shadow Pool
63.Description: When a non-boss monster dissipates, it creates a 3-meter radius poison pool that deals damage to players and heals monsters.

64.Name: Price of Power
64.Description: The character gains a 40% increase in damage and a 25% CD reduction, but loses 2% of Max HP per second. This effect will not reduce the character's health to zero.

65.Name: Roar
65.Description: Non-boss monsters become enraged when their health drops to 30%, increasing their damage by 100% until defeated.

66.Name: Absolute Defense
66.Description: All monsters gain permanent super armor.

67.Name: Frozen Domain
67.Description: Upon entering combat, circular frozen areas will randomly appear on the ground. Characters within these areas will have their Speed -25% and take damage equal to 5% of their Max HP per second. After 5s, the area will explode, dealing damage equal to 25% of Max HP to players within the area and freezing them for 5s.

68.Name: Entangling Vines
68.Description: Upon entering combat, ring-shaped vine areas will periodically appear under random characters. Characters will take damage and have their Speed -50% when entering or leaving these areas.

71.Name: Ascension (Normal)
71.Description: Monsters gain 20% additional health and have a 30% chance to increase damage by 25% when dealing damage.

72.Name: Ascension (Elite)
72.Description: Elite monsters gain 50% additional health and their damage +100%.

73.Name: Rock Solid
73.Description: Non-World Boss monsters take 30% less Crit DMG.

75.Name: Tenacity
75.Description: All damage taken by non-World Boss monsters -25%

78.Name: Divine Gift
78.Description: When the character heals, it applies a 3-second effect to the target that increases Crit Rate, Crit DMG, and ATK SPD by 25%.

79.Name: Pursue Victory
79.Description: Elite and above monsters take 50% less damage when in a super armor state, but take 500% damage when in a weakened state.

80.Name: Void Heart
80.Description: Enemies infected by Void Corruption provide a higher score bonus. In Fate mode, Void Corruption containers appear in the scene, and interacting with them infects nearby enemies with Void Seeds.

81.Name: Void Corruption Hardening
81.Description: Small monsters infected by Void Corruption are immune to control effects, and elites have their tenacity bar doubled.

82.Name: Void Corruption Eruption
82.Description: Enemies infected by Void Corruption periodically erupt with [Void Corruption Pillars].

83.Name: Weakening Link
83.Description: Every once in a while, a random player will be connected to the farthest player nearby for 10s. While the connection exists, the damage caused by both players will be continuously reduced. The connection is cleared when the players get closer to each other.

84.Name: Void Prism
84.Description: There is a chance that an untargetable Void Crystal will spawn near enemies in combat, continuously healing nearby enemies. The crystal is destroyed after a player stays near it for more than 2s.

85.Name: Void Corruption Afterimage
85.Description: There is a chance that a Void Corruption Soulshard will spawn near enemies in combat, casting for 10s. When the casting ends, it reduces the ATK of all nearby players by 50% for 10s.

86.Name: Tenacity
86.Description: Health of minor and elite enemies +30%, damage +10%

88.Name: Burning Heart
88.Description: At regular intervals, four players are randomly afflicted with Heat for 3s. Moving during this state causes HP loss.

89.Name: Shockwave
89.Description: At regular intervals, shock zones will periodically appear beneath three randomly selected players. When the zones explode, they deal DMG and cause stun to other players in range.

90.Name: Unexpected Surprise
90.Description: At regular intervals, two players will randomly receive bombs that explode after 5s, dealing AoE DMG and knockback. Jumping leaves the bomb behind at the jump location

93.Name: Void Seed
93.Description:
Some dungeon enemies are infected with [Void Seed]. Enemies infected by [Void Seed] gain multiple buffs.
Defeating enhanced enemies will earn you more points!

101.Name: Dark Corruption
101.Description: When enemies infected with Void Corruption are eliminated, they apply [Dark Corruption] to nearby players, dealing damage equal to 1% of Max HP per second per stack.

102.Name: Void Corruption Afterimage
102.Description: Periodically generates six Void Corruption Afterimages, attempting to deal high damage to players. Interrupting their chanting or controlling them will cause them to dissipate immediately and provide players with a Crit bonus.

103.Name: Enhanced Void Corruption Afterimages
103.Description: The number of Void Corruption Afterimages appearing increases to ten.

104.Name: Void Corruption Meteor
104.Description: Periodically a Void Corruption Meteor falls from the sky, dealing high shared damage in an area and providing a haste attribute bonus to players. If no player shares the damage, the meteor destroys the ground, dealing high damage to the entire team.

105.Name: Enhanced Void Corruption Meteor
105.Description: Damage caused by Void Corruption Meteor significantly increased

106.Name: Illusion Invasion
106.Description: Periodically generates an illusion that is immune to control and can restore health based on the actual damage caused, continuously attacking players and attempting to unleash an ultimate move that deals high damage to the entire team. If the illusion is destroyed, it provides players with a Mastery bonus.

107.Name: Enhanced Illusion Invasion
107.Description: The attributes of the illusion are greatly increased.

108.Name: Dispersed Energy
108.Description: Periodically generates a Fragile Star Core, scattering Star Core Energy. Picking up Star Core Energy or directly healing the soul to full health will cause it to dissipate and provide players with an Versatility bonus, otherwise it will deal high damage to players.

109.Name: Enhanced Dispersed Energy
109.Description: Initial health of the Fragile Star Core significantly decreased

110.Name: Void Corruption Afterimage
110.Description: Periodically generates six Void Corruption Afterimages, attempting to deal high damage to players. Interrupting their chanting or controlling them will cause them to dissipate immediately and provide players with a critical attribute bonus.

111.Name: Void Corruption Meteor
111.Description: Periodically a Void Corruption Meteor falls from the sky, dealing high shared damage in an area and providing a haste attribute bonus to players. If no player shares the damage, the meteor destroys the ground, dealing high damage to the entire team.

112.Name: Illusion Invasion
112.Description: Periodically generates an illusion that is immune to control and can restore health based on the actual damage caused, continuously attacking players and attempting to unleash an ultimate move that deals high damage to the entire team. If the illusion is destroyed, it provides players with a Mastery bonus.

113.Name: Dispersed Energy
113.Description: Periodically generates a Fragile Star Core, scattering Star Core Energy. Picking up Star Core Energy or directly healing the soul to full health will cause it to dissipate and provide players with an all-round attribute bonus, otherwise it will deal high damage to players.

114.Name: Bane Lord
114.Description:
Caution! The powerful Bane Lord may rarely tear through space-time and invade this dimension.
The team that loots it will obtain the high-tier loot reward corresponding to the dungeon upon completion!

116.Name: "Unit X"
116.Description: There is a "Unit X" in a fixed position in the scene, interacting with it can provide buffs!

117.Name: Bane Lord
117.Description:
Caution! The powerful Bane Lord may rarely tear through space-time and invade this dimension.
The party that loots it will obtain the high-tier loot reward corresponding to the dungeon upon completion!

118.Name: Bane Lord
118.Description:
Caution! The powerful Bane Lord may rarely tear through space-time and invade this dimension.
The party that loots it will obtain the high-tier loot reward corresponding to the dungeon upon completion!

119.Name: Power of Unity
119.Description: When the party includes Guardian, Support, and Attack classes, the party's DMG dealt +25%.

120.Name: Void Vanguard
120.Description: Empowered by the primal Void Corruption Power, all enemies in the dungeon gain significant stat boosts.

121.Name: Source of Illusion
121.Description: Empowered by the Power of Illusory Blooms, all enemies in the dungeon gain significant stat boosts.

122.Name: Illusory Performer
122.Description: Influenced by Illusion, all enemies in the Dungeon gain significant stat boosts.

201.Name: Dreamflood Manifestation
201.Description: When Dreamflood Value reaches maximum, a Dreamflood Manifestation appears to attack the player. Defeating it grants a large combat bonus.

202.Name: Illusory Energy Crystal Cluster
202.Description: Interact with Illusory Energy Crystal Clusters to greatly increase Dreamflood Value.

203.Name: Dreamflood Manifestation: Rage
203.Description: When Dreamflood Value reaches maximum, a Dreamflood Manifestation: Rage appears to attack the player. Defeating it grants a large combat bonus.

204.Name: Dreamflood Manifestation: Rupture
204.Description: When Dreamflood Value reaches maximum, a Dreamflood Manifestation: Rupture appears to attack the player. Defeating it grants a large combat bonus.

205.Name: Dreamflood Manifestation: Hunt
205.Description: When Dreamflood Value reaches maximum, a Dreamflood Manifestation: Hunt appears to attack the player. Defeating it grants a large combat bonus.

206.Name: Dreamflood Manifestation: Salvation
206.Description: When Dreamflood Value reaches maximum, a Dreamflood Manifestation: Salvation appears. Healing it grants a large combat bonus.

301.Name: Seed of Illusion
301.Description:
Certain enemies in the dungeon spawn Seed of Illusion, slightly enhancing their attributes.
Defeating enemies with Seed of Illusion grants additional Echo Residue!

302.Name: Echo Movement
302.Description: After the dungeon opens, Echo Residue can be gained by defeating monsters and triggering special events. Once full, the Movement is activated, granting buffs to all players.

303.Name: Revelry Sound System
303.Description: There are sound devices at fixed locations in the area. Interacting with them consumes Echo Residue to manually activate the Movement. Buff effects have three tiers based on energy consumed. Nearby monsters with Seed of Illusion are further enhanced and grant additional points when defeated. The Movement no longer activates automatically, except during boss battles.

311.Name: Fantastical Notes
311.Description: Every now and then, a Note Field will appear around a random player, who can pull it along to move. When Elite or higher monsters are defeated within the Note Field, they drop Note Particles. Picking them up grants additional Echo Residue.

312.Name: Spotlight Moment
312.Description: Every once in a while, a Shining Goblin will appear in the area. Defeating it grants Echo Residue. When the disco ball above its head flashes, make sure to turn your back in time!

313.Name: Dedication Dance
313.Description: Every once in a while, a Dancing Ee-chan will appear near players, causing continuous HP loss while providing Echo Residue. Healing it will grant more bonuses!

997.Name: Mark for -PT difficulty
997.Description: Used for pre-dungeon season performance, to distinguish difficulty

998.Name: Mark for -H difficulty
998.Description: Used for pre-dungeon season performance, to distinguish difficulty

999.Name: Mark for -M difficulty
999.Description: Used for pre-dungeon season performance, to distinguish difficulty

1001.Name: Dark Realm
1001.Description: The scene is shrouded in darkness, staying in the darkness will continuously lose health, and you can avoid the darkness through light orbs in the scene.

1002.Name: Vigilant Machine
1002.Description: There are patrolling robots in the area. When they detect an enemy nearby, they will rush towards them and self-destruct

1003.Name: Watcher
1003.Description: The Watcher continuously scans the area, being scanned will result in a fatal attack.

1004.Name: Purification Light Curtain
1004.Description: Purification Light Curtains appear periodically. Activate your shield to avoid DMG.

2001.Description:
Caution! The powerful Bane Lord may rarely tear through space-time and invade this dimension. The party that attacks and loots it will additionally obtain upon completion
Drop:
Lv.40 Legendary: Gauntlets, Earrings

Rare:
Lv.40 Legendary: Weapon

2061.Name: Nappo's Pandemonium
2061.Description: The mischievous Nappo sneaks into the plane, causing all kinds of trouble! The team that attacks and claims its loot will receive an additional reward upon completion: <style="ItemQuality_4">Lv.120 Legendary: Gauntlets, Earrings</style>

2062.Name: Nappo's Pandemonium
2062.Description: The mischievous Nappo sneaks into the plane, causing all kinds of trouble! The team that attacks and claims its loot will receive an additional reward upon completion: <style="ItemQuality_4">Lv.120 Legendary: Gauntlets, Earrings</style>

2101.Name: Slumberdream Mask - Raving
2101.Description:
Boss ATK +30%, HP +20%;
[Slumberdream Sigils] dropped by Bosses are guaranteed to be Epic quality.

2102.Name: Slumberdream Mask - Somniloquy
2102.Description:
When a player's HP is not full, they take +30% additional damage;
When a player's HP is full, their damage taken is decreased by -30%.

2104.Name: Slumberdream Mask - Betrayal
2104.Description:
Elite HP and Boss HP +100%, ATK +50%;
Drop rate of [Slumberdream Remnants] from Elites and Bosses +60%.

2105.Name: Slumberdream Mask - Nightmare
2105.Description:
Monsters are affected by Void Corruption (HP +20%, ATK +20%;
Drop rate of [Slumberdream Sigils] from monsters is doubled.

2106.Name: Slumberdream Mask - Snicker
2106.Description:
Player HP cannot exceed 50% of Max HP;
Monster Max HP -30%

2107.Name: Slumberdream Mask - Veiled Moon
2107.Description:
While out of combat, [Slumberdream Value] gain rate +100%;
While in combat, [Slumberdream Value] gain rate −20%

2108.Name: Slumberdream Mask - Echo
2108.Description:
For each unit, damage taken is stored and then settled every 5 seconds;
Damage taken by monsters +15%

2109.Name: Slumberdream Mask - Finale
2109.Description:
Monster ATK and HP +10%; Elites and stronger monsters gain an additional 10%;
[Slumberdream Remnants] gained from combat 40%

2110.Name: Slumberdream Mask - Symmetry
2110.Description:
Monsters gain [Protection]: DMG Reduction +50%. Each time damage is taken, this effect −0.5%;
Damage taken by players −30%

2201.Name: Boarlet's Noise Heist
2201.Description: The Squeaky Boarlet has broken through the planar barrier, hammering out chaotic beats with off-key notes! The party that attacks and claims its loot will receive additional rewards upon completion: <style="ItemQuality_4">Lv.220 Legendary Gauntlets, Earrings</style>.

5001.Name: Illegal Construction
5001.Description: Renovations are underway at Ocean Hill. Be aware: <style="accent-gn">numerous illegal buildings</style> may appear in the area!

5002.Name: Concert
5002.Description: An idol is coming to Ocean Hill for a concert. Be aware: <style="accent-gn">crowds will increase significantly</style> in the area!

5003.Name: Energy Overflow
5003.Description:
Entry gameplay will be available in future versions.

Illusion Energy is overflowing—spawn rate +100%!

5004.Name: Biting Cold Wind
5004.Description:
Entry gameplay will be available in future versions.

Winter is coming! Once the game starts, a freezing wind will blow every 35 seconds, forcing movement and removing all Illusion abilities.

5005.Name: Flash of Insight
5005.Description:
Entry gameplay will be available in future versions.

Brain overdrive! A Flash of Insight guarantees an S-Rank buff!

5006.Name: Spacetime Distortion
5006.Description:
Entry gameplay will be available in future versions.

The Illusion has been unstable lately, causing numerous space-time rifts. Entering a rift will teleport you to a random location within the scene.
